---
title: "Homer1"
author: "Trish"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:



```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)

```
```{r load delfi files}
delfi <- list.files("C:/Users/pquin/OneDrive - National University of Ireland, Galway/Research Project/FinaleDB/delfiStyleData", pattern = "tsv$", full.names = TRUE)

delfi <- delfi %>%
  set_names(basename(.)) %>%
  map_dfr(
    ~ read.delim(.x), 
    .id = "file"
  )

#Adding cancer and control labels to dataframe

delfi <- delfi %>%
  mutate(
    treatment = case_when(
      str_detect(file, "Ascite") ~ "Cancer",
      str_detect(file, "PF") ~ "Control"
    )
  )

```
```{r making bed file for homer}

#name chromosome coloumn
delfi <- delfi %>% 
  rename(chr = X.contig)

#getting the mean for each treatment in each bin
delfi <- delfi %>%
  group_by(chr, start, stop, treatment) %>%summarise(mean_ratio = mean(ratio_corrected)) %>%
  ungroup()

#finding the difference between mean cancer and control values
delfi_bins_diff <- delfi %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from =treatment,
    values_from = mean_ratio
  ) %>%
  mutate(difference = Cancer - Control, abs_difference = abs(difference)) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_difference)
  )

#choosing those delfi bins with the biggest difference
interesting_bins <- delfi_bins_diff %>%
  slice_max(abs_difference, prop = 0.1)

#converting to a bed file
homer_bed <- interesting_bins %>%
  mutate(
    chr = paste0("chr", chr)
  ) %>%
  select(chr, start, stop)
  
#saving in the correct format
write.table(
homer_bed,
file = "selection_of_delfi_bins_for_homer.bed",
sep = "\t",
quote = FALSE, 
row.names = FALSE,
col.names = FALSE

)

```
